---
title: "Attribution Models: Same Journey, Different Conclusions"
subtitle: "Which Channel Actually Drove the Sale?"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    transition: fade
    slide-number: true
    fontsize: 1.8em
    margin: 0.08
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---



## The Problem {background-color="#F4F4F4"}
```{=html}
<div style="display:flex; justify-content:center; align-items:flex-start; gap:16px; margin-top:1.5em;">
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <div style="width:150px;height:150px;border-radius:50%;background:#1877F2;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;">
      <img src="https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/b/b9/2023_Facebook_icon.svg" style="width:80px;height:80px;">
    </div>
    <div style="margin-top:0.8em;font-weight:bold;color:#222;">Facebook Ad</div>
  </div>
  <div style="font-size:2.5em;color:#aaa;margin-top:50px;">→</div>
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <div style="width:150px;height:150px;border-radius:50%;background:white;border:3px solid #eee;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;">
      <img src="https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/2/2f/Google_2015_logo.svg" style="width:100px;height:40px;">
    </div>
    <div style="margin-top:0.8em;font-weight:bold;color:#222;">Google Ads</div>
  </div>
  <div style="font-size:2.5em;color:#aaa;margin-top:50px;">→</div>
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <div style="width:150px;height:150px;border-radius:50%;background:#34A853;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;font-size:3.5em;">📧</div>
    <div style="margin-top:0.8em;font-weight:bold;color:#222;">Email Click</div>
  </div>
  <div style="font-size:2.5em;color:#aaa;margin-top:50px;">→</div>
  <div style="text-align:center;">
    <div style="width:150px;height:150px;border-radius:50%;background:#F4A700;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;font-size:3.5em;">💰</div>
    <div style="margin-top:0.8em;font-weight:bold;color:#222;">Purchase</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

::: {style="text-align: center; margin-top: 1.2em; font-size: 1.1em; color: #1a3a6b;"}
**The data aren't good enough to tell us who gets credit.**
:::

::: {style="text-align: center; margin-top: 0.6em; font-size: 0.9em; color: #444444;"}
So we pick a model — and assign credit as close to reality as we can.
:::

---


## 5 Attribution Models {background-color="#F4F4F4"}

```{=html}
<div style="display:flex; justify-content:center; gap:10px; margin-top:0.5em;">
  <div style="background:#9E9E9E;border-radius:12px;padding:12px 10px;width:130px;text-align:center;color:white;">
    <div style="font-weight:bold;margin-top:6px;font-size:0.95em;">First-touch</div>
    <div style="font-size:0.7em;margin-top:4px;opacity:0.9;">100% to first touch</div>
  </div>
  <div style="background:#757575;border-radius:12px;padding:12px 10px;width:130px;text-align:center;color:white;">
    <div style="font-weight:bold;margin-top:6px;font-size:0.95em;">Last-touch</div>
    <div style="font-size:0.7em;margin-top:4px;opacity:0.9;">100% to last touch</div>
  </div>
  <div style="background:#9E9E9E;border-radius:12px;padding:12px 10px;width:130px;text-align:center;color:white;">
    <div style="font-weight:bold;margin-top:6px;font-size:0.95em;">Linear</div>
    <div style="font-size:0.7em;margin-top:4px;opacity:0.9;">Equal split across all</div>
  </div>
  <div style="background:#757575;border-radius:12px;padding:12px 10px;width:130px;text-align:center;color:white;">
    <div style="font-weight:bold;margin-top:6px;font-size:0.95em;">Time Decay</div>
    <div style="font-size:0.7em;margin-top:4px;opacity:0.9;">More credit near conversion</div>
  </div>
  <div style="background:#9E9E9E;border-radius:12px;padding:12px 10px;width:130px;text-align:center;color:white;">
    <div style="font-weight:bold;margin-top:6px;font-size:0.95em;">U-shape</div>
    <div style="font-size:0.7em;margin-top:4px;opacity:0.9;">40% · 20% · 40%</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

models = ['First-touch', 'Last-touch', 'Linear', 'Time Decay', 'Position-based']
facebook = [1000,    0, 333, 160, 400]
google   = [   0,    0, 333, 260, 200]
email    = [   0, 1000, 334, 580, 400]

x = np.arange(len(models))
width = 0.55

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 3.2))
fig.patch.set_facecolor('#E8E8E8')
ax.set_facecolor('#E8E8E8')

ax.bar(x, facebook, width, label='Facebook', color='#4A90D9')
ax.bar(x, google,   width, bottom=facebook, label='Google', color='#5BAD6F')
ax.bar(x, email,    width, bottom=[f+g for f,g in zip(facebook,google)], label='Email', color='#E6A817')

ax.set_xticks(x)
ax.set_xticklabels(models, color='#222222', fontsize=11)
ax.set_ylabel('Attributed Conversions', color='#222222', fontsize=10)
ax.set_ylim(0, 1100)
ax.tick_params(colors='#222222')
ax.yaxis.set_tick_params(labelcolor='#222222')
ax.spines[:].set_color('#cccccc')
ax.legend(loc='upper right', fontsize=9, framealpha=0.3, labelcolor='#222222')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('channel_chart.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight', facecolor='#E8E8E8')
plt.show()
```

::: {style="font-size:0.8em; color:#1a3a6b; margin-top:0.3em; text-align:center;"}
Facebook credit: **0 → 1,000** depending on model chosen. Same data. Very different story.
:::

---

## How Do We Know Which Model Is Better? {background-color="#E8E8E8"}

```{=html}
<div style="margin-top:1.2em; font-size:0.88em;">
  <div style="background:#e8f0fb;border-left:5px solid #4A90D9;padding:16px 20px;border-radius:6px;margin-bottom:14px;">
    <strong>💡 Spend Validation</strong>
    <div style="display:flex; gap:20px; margin-top:0.8em;">
      <div style="flex:1; background:#f0f7f0; border-radius:10px; padding:12px; border-left:4px solid #5BAD6F;">
        <div style="font-weight:bold; color:#5BAD6F; margin-bottom:6px;">✅ Good Model</div>
        <svg viewBox="0 0 200 80" style="width:100%;">
          <polyline points="10,60 50,40 90,50 130,20 170,30" fill="none" stroke="#4A90D9" stroke-width="2.5"/>
          <polyline points="10,65 50,45 90,55 130,25 170,35" fill="none" stroke="#E6A817" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-dasharray="4,2"/>
          <text x="10" y="78" font-size="9" fill="#4A90D9">Spend</text>
          <text x="60" y="78" font-size="9" fill="#E6A817">── Attribution</text>
        </svg>
        <div style="font-size:0.78em; color:#555; margin-top:4px;">Spend ↑ → Attribution ↑</div>
      </div>
      <div style="flex:1; background:#fdf2f2; border-radius:10px; padding:12px; border-left:4px solid #E07B4F;">
        <div style="font-weight:bold; color:#E07B4F; margin-bottom:6px;">❌ Poor Model</div>
        <svg viewBox="0 0 200 80" style="width:100%;">
          <polyline points="10,60 50,30 90,50 130,20 170,40" fill="none" stroke="#4A90D9" stroke-width="2.5"/>
          <polyline points="10,45 50,47 90,44 130,46 170,45" fill="none" stroke="#E6A817" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-dasharray="4,2"/>
          <text x="10" y="78" font-size="9" fill="#4A90D9">Spend</text>
          <text x="60" y="78" font-size="9" fill="#E6A817">── Attribution</text>
        </svg>
        <div style="font-size:0.78em; color:#555; margin-top:4px;">Spend fluctuates → Attribution flat</div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div style="background:#e8f4ec;border-left:5px solid #5BAD6F;padding:16px 20px;border-radius:6px;">
    <strong>🧪 Incrementality Testing</strong><br>
    <span style="color:#444;">Randomly withhold a channel from a control group. Measure the conversion difference.</span><br>
    <span style="color:#888;font-size:0.9em;">A form of A/B testing — the gold standard for measuring true channel impact.</span>
  </div>
</div>
```

::: {style="text-align:center; font-size:0.8em; color:#888; margin-top:1em;"}
Both methods help us move from *assuming* to *validating*.
:::
---

## Takeaway {background-color="#E8E8E8"}

::: {style="text-align: center; font-size: 1.1em; margin-top: 1em; line-height: 1.8; color:#333;"}
The data alone can't tell us who drove the sale.

So we pick a model — and **validate it.**
:::

::: {style="text-align: center; font-size: 0.95em; color: #555555; margin-top: 1em;"}
Use **spend validation** as a quick sanity check.<br>
Use **incrementality testing** when you need the real answer.
:::

::: {style="text-align: center; font-size: 0.88em; color:#1a3a6b; margin-top:1em; font-weight:bold;"}
Attribution models assign credit.<br>
Only experiments can prove it.
:::
